source: Zylos Research — Graceful Degradation Patterns in AI Agent Systems
summary: Comprehensive survey of layered recovery patterns for production AI agents: Level 0 (retry) → Level 1 (rephrase) → Level 2 (reroute model) → Level 3 (replan) → Level 4 (escalate). Includes circuit-breaker and bulkhead patterns.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — maps directly to the AIBlocked gap; ProcessRunner has no retry today. Inspired HID-117.

summary: Dapr 2026 agent resiliency: exponential backoff with jitter for subprocess retries, finite-state circuit breaker, and self-healing state machines that automate recovery from transient failures.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — concrete implementation patterns for ProcessRunner retry loop. Co-inspired HID-117.

summary: Accepted AAAI 2026. Selects optimal LLM set via Integer Linear Programming + RL-based topology, achieving 86% cost reduction at comparable accuracy across 3 benchmarks. Requires historical per-agent cost/performance data.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — the foundational motivation for building a per-stage cost ledger; BAMAS needs the telemetry data that HID-118 would produce. Inspired HID-118.

source: Agent Cost Metrics: Per-Task, Per-User Framework 2026 (digitalapplied.com)
summary: Practical framework for tracking LLM cost at per-task and per-stage granularity with three operational rhythms: daily engineering, weekly product-finance, quarterly strategic.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — provides the metric schema and aggregation patterns for HID-118 (per-stage telemetry endpoint). Co-inspired HID-118.

summary: Coding agent that improves its own codebase autonomously. ICLR 2025 workshop paper. Achieves 17–53% improvement on SWE-bench Verified by reflecting on failures and editing its own agent code. Keep-best loop with objective verifiability constraint.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — maps to the no-agent-memory gap. AgentForge already has AgentSkill+DistillationAgent infrastructure but no failure-path reflection. Inspired HID-119.

summary: 630-line Python tool that runs ML experiments autonomously overnight (700 experiments / 2 days), keeping only changes that beat the current best. Keep-best loop with objective metrics. 21k GitHub stars in days.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — conceptually aligns with failure reflection (keep-best), but targets ML training loops not code orchestration. Partially co-inspired HID-119.

summary: DSP: async online RL framework that dynamically predicts when to speculate next steps during idle LLM thinking time. No pre-training needed. Optimizes joint latency+cost objective via biased step prediction. Lossless acceleration.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — AgentForge is sequential single-agent-per-stage; speculative planning requires parallel execution branches which would conflict with the deterministic state machine. Very novel but architecturally misaligned.

summary: Pre-executes likely future tool calls during LLM thinking time using a shadow queue ranked by utility. 48.5% reduction in task completion time, 1.8x tool throughput on SWE-bench and MetaGPT workloads.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — impressive latency results but AgentForge executes CLI subprocesses (not tool calls) and is bounded by human approval gates, not raw tool latency. Architecturally misaligned.

source: AI Agent Memory Architectures 2026 — Mem0, Zep Graphiti, Letta (zylos.ai + mem0.ai)
summary: State of AI agent memory 2026: three-tier taxonomy (episodic/semantic/procedural), leading frameworks Mem0 (48k stars), Zep Graphiti (temporal KG), LangMem, Letta. Vector+graph hybrid emerging as dominant pattern.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge's AgentSkill table provides procedural memory, but no episodic (run-specific history injection) or semantic (entity relationship) memory. HID-119 partially addresses procedural; episodic/semantic would be a separate future issue.

source: AI Agent Workflow Checkpointing and Resumability (zylos.ai 2026)
summary: Checkpointing saves complete agent execution state at each completed step, enabling mid-task resume after failure. LangGraph, Temporal, Dagster all ship first-class checkpoint primitives in 2026. 15-30% of long-running tasks fail mid-execution.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly maps to the no-plan-repair gap (RESET_TO_TODO loses all progress). Strong candidate for a future issue; not selected this run because it requires deeper Prisma schema changes and state machine extensions than the selected issues.

source: Kitchen Loop: User-Spec-Driven Development for a Self-Evolving Codebase (arxiv:2603.25697)
summary: Adversarial verification interposed between LLM code generation and merge: a verification agent checks generated code against live product state before committing. Addresses verifiability constraint for self-improving agents.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — the adversarial verification concept aligns with AgentForge's existing plan review stage (Codex reviews before human approval). Novel framing but the capability already partially exists. Too early for a distinct issue.
